texto: I asked God "Why, why, why?" I turned my face away and wished that I were imagining it all. I had tasted the bitterest essence of war, the sight of helpless comrades being slaughtered, and it filled me with disgust.” ― Eugene B. Sledge, With the Old Breed: At Peleliu and Okinawa
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Eugene Sledge que provoca temas ligados a humor. Ao questionar “I asked God "Why, why, why?" I turned my face away and wished that I were imagining it all. I had…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre humor, the-pacific, war, com foco em war.
